description: Luck: the part of any outcome that no one earned, good or otherwise. Phi keeps it value-neutral; 'soleha' is the way things fall, not a blessing and not a verdict.
sound_symbolism: Bright 'so' opening to 'le'-'ha', light and unweighted throughout: what arrives without being carried.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'thia soleha phelu.' (You have luck.) 'mia soleha phaelo.' (I feel fortunate.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Naming luck is naming the limits of merit: much of what happens was never up to anyone. The word keeps that humility speakable.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Farmers knew exactly how much of a harvest was skill and how much was weather. Luck was the honest name for the difference.
